goal: Create review-only March 20, 2025 Sentinel TCI raster-window previews after the matching SCL-first QA probe passed for TCI review.

method.coordinate_transform: Reuses the January 19 bbox pixel windows derived from the same Sentinel MGRS tile grids and draft WGS84 planning bounds.
method.pixel_model: Sentinel-2 TCI COGs are treated as 10980x10980 rasters at 10 m/pixel using the same tile grid as the prior 2025-01-19 bbox clip review.
method.clip_algorithm: Open the full TCI COG with Pillow, crop the native pixel window, and downsample each preview to width 1024 while preserving aspect ratio.
limitations: Uses draft planning bbox corners, not an official Pacuare route polygon, wetted channel, bank polygon, or protected-area-safe publication geometry.
Does not apply SCL, cloud, shadow, haze, nodata, water, canopy, wet-rock, waterfall, or mist QA masks to the TCI pixels.
Does not stitch the two clips, reproject into a final working CRS, or align to guide-reviewed stationing.

outputs.visual_review: Cleaner than the January 19 clip and useful for source review, but scattered clouds and cloud shadows remain visible; not route-clipped or stitch-ready.
Cleaner than the January 19 clip and useful for source review, but scattered clouds and shadows remain; not route-clipped or stitch-ready.

windowed_read_decision.decision: March 20 TCI bbox clips are better visual review candidates than January 19, but remain blocked from production until exact route/bank clipping, SCL/cloud masking, seam/stitch review, terms review, and guide/geospatial approval pass.

forbidden_use: production water or vegetation mask generation
promotion_blockers: Replace draft bbox clipping with reviewed route/bank/corridor geometry in the selected Costa Rica working CRS.
Apply SCL/cloud/shadow masks to the exact route windows and review the resulting usable coverage.
Stitch 17PKM/16PHR only after native-CRS clipping, SCL/cloud/shadow QA, seam review, and scale review pass.
Record Sentinel terms, attribution, storage/LFS policy, and redistribution review before any production derivative is used.
Compare accepted imagery against rainfall, flow, and guide/outfitter context before using river color, wetness, or bank exposure for gameplay visuals.
